context: W5.0 m365 RE-TEST. #1290 verdict FAIL was investigated by dev supervisor and is NOT a W5.0 regression: engine log (20:42:54) shows the APF engine blocked correctly — [APF:block] service=m365_copilot via M365SignalrHandler factory, block_response size=752, which is BYTE-SIZE-IDENTICAL to all prior m365 blocks (14:52 pre-W5.0 / 18:17 W4+W6 / 20:42 W5.0 all =752). M365SignalrHandler::render_envelope is a transparent passthrough that does NOT use render_envelope_v3 (the only thing W5.0 changed), so W5.0 cannot affect m365's output. The #1290 FAIL ('Something went wrong' generic M365 error + no 차단) is the known m365 SignalR-inject client-render fragility (Task#7/#8), path-dependent: the W4+W6 baseline #1269 succeeded via the page-replacement (Option C) path, while #1290 hit the SignalR-inject path. This re-test confirms m365 can still render the 차단 warning on the W5.0 binary.
notes: Logged-in m365 (user 최장희). Submit the PII prompt; wait for the block warning to render (either via page-replacement '보안 정책 - 접근 차단' page OR an in-chat 차단 SignalR bubble). PASS = '차단' rendered. If 'Something went wrong' recurs, hard-reload m365.cloud.microsoft/chat once more (page-replacement Option C should fire on the post-block page load within 5min). Report engine_intercept_fired + which path rendered. NOT-a-W5.0-regression already established via engine byte-identical 752; this is confirmatory.
